author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
āmi ta svānanda⋅sukhadavāsī
rādhikāmādhava⋅caraṇadāsī

du̐hāra milane ānanda kari
du̐hāra viyoge duḥkhete mari

sakhīsthalī nāhi heri nayane
dekhile śaibyāke paḍaye mane

je⋅ye pratikūla candrāra sakhī
prāṇe duḥkha pāi tāhāre dekhi’

rādhikā⋅kuñja ā̐dhāra kari’
loite cāhe se rādhāra hari

śrī⋅rādhā⋅govinda⋅milana⋅sukha
pratikūlajana nā heri mukha

rādhā⋅pratikūla jateka jana
sambhāṣaṇe kabhu nā haya mana

bhakativinoda śrī⋅rādhācaraṇe
sa̐peche parāṇa atīva jatane
translation_intro: From The Harmonist, January 1929. Edited by Bhaktisiddhanta Sarasvati.
en_translation:
I, indeed, am a denizen of the grove called the pleasaunce of the joy of my own self, A hand-maid tending the feet of Radhika and Madhaba.

I rejoice at the union of the Divine Pair, And die by grief at separation that parts them.

I never cast my glance on the place of pastime of the maidens who are their confidential associates; I am reminded of Shaibya whenever I behold the site.

My heart is smitten with grief as I see those Who are the confidantes of Chandra opposed to Radhika.

She wants to take away Hari of Radha, Causing the grove of Radhika to be overcast with the darkness of gloom!

I never see the faces of those Who are opposed to the joy of the union of Sri Radha Govinda.

Never do I find any pleasure In greeting those who are opposed to Radha.

(Bhaktivinoda, at the feet of Sri Radha, Hath surrendered his very life-breath with the utmost care.)
